tools.name: rdev.support_session.connect
tools.description: Single high-level Agent entry for 'connect a computer'. Fresh Agents should call this before rdev.invites.create, rdev.connection_entry.plan, hand-written gateway setup, or package materialization. If a reachable or configured gateway exists, creates the attended-temporary session and returns user_handoff, agent_connection_runbook, gateway_candidate_preflight, connectivity_helper_preflight, connection_supervision, and status watcher in one payload. If no gateway is running, returns cli_start_now_command plus agent_connection_runbook for the standard visible foreground rdev support-session connect --start flow, instead of making the Agent choose lower-level handoff/create/start/status steps. The created target command and host runtime keep signed gateway candidates so target bootstrap and post-registration job transport can fail over inside rdev. Read agent_connection_runbook.fresh_agent_failure_prevention before writing setup code; it captures known failure patterns such as manual gateway/invite/bootstrap glue, missing helper assets that produce 'rdev is required', background gateway workarounds, approval polling, and Agent-written PowerShell/shell scripts.
tools.safety: Creates a scoped attended-temporary ticket only when a reachable/configured gateway is supplied; otherwise planning only. Does not execute on the target host, install hidden persistence, bypass policy, or mutate firewall/DNS/routes.

tools.name: rdev.invites.create
tools.description: Create an Agent-first remote session invite with a universal connection entry, manifest URL, pinned manifest root, transport plan, and next MCP actions. For ordinary 'connect this computer' requests, call rdev.support_session.connect first; use invites.create for reviewed package materialization, managed owned-host planning, or explicit lower-level workflows.
tools.safety: Creates an access path and connection metadata; does not execute on the host.

tools.name: rdev.support_session.handoff
tools.description: Return the single standard first-contact decision for a fresh Agent that needs to connect a target machine, including agent_connection_runbook and fresh_agent_failure_prevention. If gateway_url is provided or a configured RDEV_*_GATEWAY_URL fallback exists, call rdev.support_session.create next; if not, run cli_start_now_command, the visible foreground rdev support-session connect --start command, then read its ready_file.path for the handoff and status_file.path for the latest machine-readable connection event when terminal output is hard to parse. Agents should use this before choosing between prepare/create/start/plan/status, and must not write their own bootstrap, relay, approval-polling, or recovery scripts.
tools.safety: Planning only; does not create tickets, start a gateway, approve hosts, execute on the target host, or install persistence.

tools.name: rdev.support_session.prepare
tools.description: Inspect a fresh Agent runtime for one-command support-session readiness: local rdev recovery, Go/Git checkout state, gateway URL candidates, agent_connection_runbook, fresh_agent_failure_prevention, gateway_candidate_preflight, connectivity_helper_preflight, configured RDEV_*_GATEWAY_URL fallbacks, platform helper asset availability, target bootstrap self-repair, and standard recovery actions. Agents should call this before improvising setup when rdev or gateway state is unclear, then use agent_connection_runbook, gateway_candidate_preflight, connectivity_helper_preflight, and the recommended gateway_url_candidates entry instead of asking humans to assemble gateway values or writing custom probes.
tools.safety: Read-only by default; with build_assets=true it builds local helper binaries from the checked-out source but does not create tickets, start a gateway, approve hosts, or execute on a target machine.

tools.name: rdev.support_session.create
tools.description: Create an attended temporary support session through an already reachable gateway and return a ready user_handoff, target command, status watcher, agent_connection_runbook, fresh_agent_failure_prevention, gateway_candidate_preflight, connectivity_helper_preflight, connection_supervision, and connection_continuity_policy. If gateway_url is omitted, the tool uses the first configured RDEV_*_GATEWAY_URL fallback. The target command is the standardized fallback surface and can try ordered gateway URL candidates, including configured RDEV_*_GATEWAY_URL hosted/relay/mesh/VPN/SSH fallbacks, with bounded timeout/retry behavior without Agent-authored scripts. After registration, rdev host serve --transport auto can reuse signed join-manifest gateway candidates and switch to another reachable gateway when the current path fails before jobs are processed. Agents should follow agent_connection_runbook first, read fresh_agent_failure_prevention before writing setup code, read gateway_candidate_preflight and connectivity_helper_preflight before asking humans or writing probes, read connection_supervision to wait, report connected=true, and choose standard upgrade/recovery tools; read stable_after_lan_change before promising durable connectivity, prefer user_handoff.message plus user_handoff.copy_paste for the human-facing response, and use rdev support-session connect --start when no gateway is running instead of manually creating an invite, substituting ticket codes, or writing bootstrap glue.
tools.safety: Creates a scoped attended-temporary ticket with first-host auto approval by default; does not execute on the target host or install hidden persistence.

tools.name: rdev.support_session.plan
tools.description: Create a standardized Agent-native support session plan for review/debug access to gateway startup, recommended gateway URL candidates, configured RDEV_*_GATEWAY_URL fallbacks, verified helper assets, invite creation, localized target commands with built-in candidate fallback/timeout policy, and scoped attended-temporary auto-approval. Agents should prefer rdev.support_session.connect, or rdev support-session connect --start when a local foreground gateway is needed.
tools.safety: Planning only; does not start a gateway, create a ticket, approve a host, install software, or execute on the target host.

tools.name: rdev.support_session.status
tools.description: Read or wait on standardized support-session connection status for a ticket code so Agents can tell the user when the target host has connected, is pending approval, timed out, or is still waiting.
tools.safety: Read-only status check; does not approve hosts or execute jobs.

tools.name: rdev.connection_entry.plan
tools.description: Materialize an invite into the universal Connection Entry handoff and self-contained Connection Entry runner package. Humans receive only a link, visible launcher, or signed package; ticket, gateway, manifest root, transport, release, relay, mesh, VPN, SSH, checksum, helper startup, and approved dependency install values stay in Agent/tool metadata.
tools.safety: Planning only; writes no remote state and does not execute on the target host.

tools.name: rdev.tickets.create
tools.description: Create a short-lived support or remote development ticket for a target host.
tools.safety: Creates access path; does not execute on host.

tools.name: rdev.tickets.revoke
tools.description: Revoke a support ticket and prevent new host enrollment through it.
tools.safety: Revocation only.

tools.name: rdev.hosts.list
tools.description: List enrolled or pending hosts visible to the operator.
tools.safety: Read-only.

tools.name: rdev.hosts.capabilities
tools.description: Inspect detected and approved capabilities for one host.
tools.safety: Read-only.

tools.name: rdev.hosts.approve
tools.description: Approve a pending host for a ticket-scoped policy.
tools.safety: Access-granting; requires operator approval.

tools.name: rdev.hosts.revoke
tools.description: Revoke host access and disconnect active sessions.
tools.safety: Revocation only.

tools.name: rdev.jobs.create
tools.description: Create a signed, policy-bound remote job for an approved host.
tools.safety: May execute on host after policy checks.

tools.name: rdev.jobs.status
tools.description: Read status and latest events for a remote job.
tools.safety: Read-only.

tools.name: rdev.jobs.cancel
tools.description: Request cooperative cancellation of a running job.
tools.safety: Cancellation only.

tools.name: rdev.jobs.approve
tools.description: Approve a pending high-risk job action such as elevation or package install.
tools.safety: Dangerous-action approval.

tools.name: rdev.artifacts.list
tools.description: List artifacts produced by a job.
tools.safety: Read-only metadata.

tools.name: rdev.artifacts.read
tools.description: Read an artifact produced by a job.
tools.safety: Read-only; may expose sensitive job output.

tools.name: rdev.artifacts.export_bundle
tools.description: Export a reviewable evidence bundle for one job, including manifest, checksums, envelope, artifacts, and audit slice.
tools.safety: Read-only evidence export; may expose sensitive job output.

tools.name: rdev.audit.query
tools.description: Query redacted audit events for tickets, hosts, and jobs.
tools.safety: Read-only audit access.

tools.name: rdev.policy.explain
tools.description: Explain whether a requested action would be allowed by policy.
tools.safety: Read-only policy simulation.

tools.name: rdev.policy.explain_shell
tools.description: Explain whether a shell job policy is structurally allowed before creating a signed job.
tools.safety: Read-only policy simulation; host still re-validates before execution.

tools.name: rdev.enrollment.verify_certificate
tools.description: Verify a host enrollment certificate and optional signed revocation list against a pinned enrollment root before trusting a host registration.
tools.safety: Read-only certificate and revocation validation; does not issue certificates or grant host access.

tools.name: rdev.update.check
tools.description: Check the latest GitHub Release for Remote Dev Skillkit and compare it with the installed version.
tools.safety: Read-only GitHub release metadata lookup; does not download, install, or replace files.

tools.name: rdev.update.plan
tools.description: Create a dry-run update plan from the latest GitHub Release, including platform archive selection and verification steps.
tools.safety: Read-only planning; does not download, install, replace binaries, restart services, or mutate configuration.

tools.name: rdev.adapter.verify_result
tools.description: Verify an adapter result artifact against the public result-artifact conformance contract.
tools.safety: Read-only artifact validation; does not execute on a host.

tools.name: rdev.adapter.verify_lifecycle
tools.description: Verify an adapter lifecycle manifest against the public Adapter SDK lifecycle conformance contract.
tools.safety: Read-only manifest validation; does not execute on a host.

tools.name: rdev.adapter.verify_cancellation
tools.description: Verify an adapter cancellation result artifact against the public cancellation conformance contract.
tools.safety: Read-only artifact validation; does not execute on a host.

tools.name: rdev.adapter.verify_runtime
tools.description: Verify an adapter runtime fixture generated by the public Adapter SDK lifecycle runner.
tools.safety: Read-only fixture validation; does not execute on a host.
